label: Quran · Surah
question: I am the opening of the Quran, recited in every raka'ah. My name means 'The Opening'.
answer: Surah Al-Fatiha — Surah 1, 7 verses

label: Quran · Ayah
question: 'In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious...' — complete this.
answer: ...the Most Merciful (Ar-Raheem) — Al-Fatiha 1:1

label: Quran · Surah
question: I describe the Night of Power (Laylat al-Qadr) — better than 1,000 months. I have only 5 verses.
answer: Surah Al-Qadr — Surah 97

label: Quran · Surah
question: I am the last two Surahs of the Quran, recited together for protection. What are we collectively called?
answer: Al-Mu'awwidhatain — Al-Falaq (113) + An-Nas (114)

label: Quran · Ayah
question: 'All praise is due to Allah, Lord of...' — second verse of Al-Fatiha.
answer: ...the worlds (al-'alameen) — 1:2

label: Quran · Ayah
question: 'Indeed we belong to Allah and...' — the phrase said upon difficulty or loss.
answer: ...to Him we shall return — Al-Baqarah 2:156

label: Quran · Surah
question: I am the shortest Surah with only 3 verses. I mention a river in Paradise.
answer: Surah Al-Kawthar — Surah 108

label: Quran · Surah
question: I am recited every Friday. My name refers to the Day of Gathering (Friday prayer).
answer: Surah Al-Jumu'ah — Surah 62

label: Quran · Ayah
question: 'And your Lord said: Call upon Me, I will...' — complete from Surah Ghafir.
answer: ...respond to you — Ghafir 40:60

label: Quran · Ayah
question: 'So verily, with the hardship, there is...' — what comes after?
answer: ...relief / ease (yusra) — Ash-Sharh 94:5

label: Quran · Ayah
question: 'He is Allah, the One...' — which Surah begins with this and what does it teach?
answer: Surah Al-Ikhlas (112:1). Teaches Tawhid. Worth 1/3 of the Quran.

label: Quran · Surah
question: I am the longest Surah with 286 ayahs. My name refers to a cow the Israelites sacrificed.
answer: Surah Al-Baqarah — Surah 2

label: Quran · Surah
question: I am named after a spider whose web protected the Prophet (pbuh) and Abu Bakr in a cave during the Hijra. I also tell the story of trials of faith.
answer: Surah Al-Ankabut / The Spider — Surah 29. The spider's web is in 29:41.

label: Quran · Surah
question: I am named after an insect that warned its colony from Sulayman's army. I mention the Queen of Sheba.
answer: Surah An-Naml / The Ant — Surah 27

label: Quran · Surah
question: I am named after a cave where young believers hid for centuries. I also contain the story of Dhul-Qarnayn.
answer: Surah Al-Kahf — Surah 18. Recommended every Friday.

label: Quran · Surah
question: I am named after a she-camel — a miraculous sign given to Prophet Salih AS for the people of Thamud.
answer: The she-camel story is in Surah Hud (11:61-68) and Ash-Shams (91:13)

label: Quran · Ayah
question: 'Indeed with hardship will be...' — complete this from Surah Ash-Sharh.
answer: ...ease (yusra) — 94:5. Repeated twice! One hardship, two eases.

label: Quran · Ayah
question: 'And I did not create jinn and humans except...' — complete from Adh-Dhariyat.
answer: ...except to worship Me — 51:56

label: Quran · Ayah
question: 'O mankind, indeed We have created you from male and female and made you peoples and tribes that you may...' — complete from Al-Hujurat.
answer: ...know one another. Indeed, the most noble of you in the sight of Allah is the most righteous — Al-Hujurat 49:13

label: Quran · Ayah
question: 'Indeed, Allah will not change the condition of a people until they change what is within...' — name the Surah.
answer: ...themselves — Surah Ar-Ra'd (The Thunder) 13:11

label: Quran · Ayah
question: 'O believers, fasting has been decreed upon you as it was decreed upon...' — complete.
answer: ...those before you, that you may become righteous — Al-Baqarah 2:183

label: Quran · Ayah
question: 'And We have certainly made the Quran easy for...' — complete from Al-Qamar.
answer: ...remembrance. So is there any who will remember? — 54:17

label: Quran · Surah
question: I contain the full story of Prophet Yusuf AS — dream, well, Egypt, prison, reunion. I am 'the best of stories'.
answer: Surah Yusuf — Surah 12. 'Ahsan al-qasas' in 12:3.

label: Quran · Surah
question: I begin with Ya-Sin. I am often described as 'the heart of the Quran' and recited for the dying.
answer: Surah Ya-Sin — Surah 36

label: Quran · Surah
question: I begin with 'Tabarak' and am said to protect from the punishment of the grave. I have 30 verses.
answer: Surah Al-Mulk — Surah 67

label: Quran · Surah
question: I contain Ayat al-Kursi, the verse on no compulsion in religion, and the longest verse in the Quran. Name me.
answer: Surah Al-Baqarah — 2:255, 2:256, 2:282

label: Quran · Surah
question: I am named after iron. I state iron was 'sent down' — which modern science confirms came from supernovae.
answer: Surah Al-Hadid / Iron — 57:25

label: Quran · Finish that Āyah
question: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ ... — Finish Āyat al-Kursī.
answer: Āyat al-Kursī — Al-Baqarah 2:255 — The greatest verse in the Quran. Recite after every obligatory prayer for protection.

ayahReveal.arabic: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ ۚ لَا تَأْخُذُهُ سِنَةٌ وَلَا نَوْمٌ ۚ لَّهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۗ مَن ذَا الَّذِي يَشْفَعُ عِندَهُ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِ ۚ يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ ۖ وَلَا يُحِيطُونَ بِشَيْءٍ مِّنْ عِلْمِهِ إِلَّا بِمَا شَاءَ ۚ وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ ۖ وَلَا يَئُودُهُ حِفْظُهُمَا ۚ وَهُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْعَظِيمُ
ayahReveal.meaning: Allah — there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Self-Sustaining. Neither drowsiness nor sleep overtakes Him. To Him belongs all in the heavens and earth. Who can intercede except by His permission? He knows what is before and behind them. They encompass nothing of His knowledge except what He wills. His Kursī extends over the heavens and earth, and preserving them does not weary Him. He is the Most High, the Most Great.

label: Quran · Ayah
question: 'Allah does not burden a soul beyond that it can bear' — name the Surah and verse number.
answer: Al-Baqarah 2:286 — last verse of the Surah

label: Quran · Ayah
question: 'Whoever puts their trust in Allah, He will be enough for them.' Name the verse and the concept.
answer: At-Talaq 65:3. The concept is Tawakkul (reliance on Allah).

label: Quran · Ayah
question: Which verse is the 'Verse of Light'? Describe what it compares Allah's light to.
answer: An-Nur 24:35. A niche with a lamp, lamp in glass, glass like a star, lit from olive tree.

label: Quran · Surah
question: I am called 'The Event.' I open with: 'When the Occurrence occurs...' I vividly describe three groups on the Day of Judgment. Name me and the three groups.
answer: Surah Al-Waqi'ah — Surah 56. 1) As-Sabiqun (the foremost) 2) Ashab al-Yameen (people of the right) 3) Ashab ash-Shimal (people of the left).

label: Quran · Ayah
question: Name the verse listing 8 categories of Zakat recipients. Which Surah and verse?
answer: At-Tawbah 9:60 — poor, needy, administrators, reconciled hearts, freeing slaves, debtors, Allah's cause, stranded traveler.

label: Sunnah · Hadith
question: 'Actions are judged by their intentions, and every person will get reward according to what they have...' Complete.
answer: ...intended. (Bukhari 1, Muslim 1907)

label: Sunnah · Hadith
question: 'Cleanliness is ___ of faith.' Complete this hadith.
answer: Half. (Muslim 223)

label: Sunnah · Hadith
question: 'None of you truly believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for...' Complete.
answer: ...himself. (Bukhari 13, Muslim 45)

label: Sunnah · Hadith
question: 'The best of you are those who learn the Quran and...' Complete.
answer: ...teach it. (Bukhari 5027)

label: Sunnah · Hadith
question: 'Smiling in the face of your brother is...' Complete.
answer: ...charity (sadaqah). (At-Tirmidhi 1956)

label: Sunnah · Hadith
question: What is the greeting of Islam? What does it mean?
answer: As-salamu alaykum — 'Peace be upon you.' (Muslim 54)

label: Sunnah · Prophet
question: What was the full name of Prophet Muhammad (pbuh)? What does 'Muhammad' mean?
answer: Muhammad ibn Abdullah. 'The Praised One.' Also called Ahmad.

label: Sunnah · Prophet
question: In which cave did the Prophet (pbuh) receive the first revelation? What were the first words?
answer: Cave Hira. 'Iqra!' (Read/Recite) — Surah Al-Alaq 96:1. Age 40.

label: Sunnah · Prophet
question: What is the Hijra and when did it occur? What does it mark?
answer: Migration from Makkah to Madinah — 622 CE. Start of Hijri calendar.

label: Sunnah · Prophet
question: Which Prophet AS was swallowed by a whale? What du'a did he make inside it?
answer: Prophet Yunus AS. 'La ilaha illa anta subhanaka inni kuntu minaz-zalimin' (21:87)

label: Sunnah · Prophet
question: What was the Prophet (pbuh)'s profession before prophethood? What was he known as?
answer: Merchant/trader. 'As-Sadiq Al-Ameen' (The Truthful, The Trustworthy).

label: Sunnah · Hadith
question: 'Religion is ___.' Fill in the ONE word.
answer: Naseeha (Sincere Advice). (Muslim 55)

label: Sunnah · Hadith
question: Name the Sahabi who narrated the most hadiths — over 5000.
answer: Abu Hurairah — ~5,374 hadiths.

label: Sunnah · Hadith
question: 'The best of you are those who are best to their ___.' Fill in the blank.
answer: Families (wives). (At-Tirmidhi 3895)

label: Sunnah · Hadith
question: Name the two hadith collections called 'The Two Sahihs.'
answer: Sahih Al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim (Al-Sahihayn).

label: Sunnah · Hadith
question: What is the Hadith of Jibril? What three levels of religion does it describe?
answer: Islam, Iman (Faith), Ihsan (Excellence). (Muslim 8)

label: Sunnah · Hadith
question: 'Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him speak good or...' Complete.
answer: ...remain silent. (Bukhari 6018, Muslim 47)

label: Sunnah · Prophet
question: What title was Prophet Ibrahim AS given? What does it mean?
answer: Khalilullah — 'Friend of Allah.' (Quran 4:125)

label: Sunnah · Prophet
question: Describe Isra wal Mi'raj. What was its most significant outcome?
answer: Night journey to Al-Aqsa + ascent through heavens. 5 daily prayers made obligatory.

label: Sunnah · Prophet
question: Battle of Badr (2 AH) — What was remarkable about the outcome?
answer: 313 Muslims defeated ~1,000 Quraysh (3:1 odds). Angels sent to assist.

label: Sunnah · Prophet
question: What was the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah? Why did the Prophet (pbuh) call it a victory?
answer: Peace treaty (6 AH). Quran called it 'a clear victory' (48:1). Islam spread massively after.

label: Sunnah · Prophet
question: Name the Prophet (pbuh)'s first wife, first daughter, and burial city.
answer: Wife: Khadijah. Daughter: Zainab. Buried in Madinah (Masjid an-Nabawi).

label: Sunnah · Hadith
question: 'The most beloved deeds to Allah are...' — what follows?
answer: ...the most consistent, even if they are small. (Bukhari 6465)

label: Sunnah · Hadith
question: Name the six rights of a Muslim upon a Muslim.
answer: 1) Return salaam 2) Accept invitations 3) Give advice 4) Yarhamukallah at sneeze 5) Visit sick 6) Follow funeral. (Muslim 2162)

label: Sunnah · Hadith
question: What are the 'Nawaqid al-Islam' (nullifiers)? Name at least four.
answer: Major shirk, mocking religion, believing another's guidance surpasses the Prophet's, supporting polytheists against Muslims, hating any part of the message.

label: Sunnah · Hadith
question: Name the six authentic hadith collections (Al-Kutub as-Sittah) and their compilers.
answer: 1) Bukhari 2) Muslim 3) Abu Dawud 4) At-Tirmidhi 5) An-Nasa'i 6) Ibn Majah

label: Sunnah · Hadith
question: 'I have been given five things not given to anyone before me...' Name at least three.
answer: 1) Victory through awe 2) Earth made a place of prayer 3) War booty lawful 4) Intercession 5) Sent to all mankind. (Bukhari 335)

label: Sunnah · Prophet
question: Name the five Ulul 'Azm (Messengers of greatest resolve).
answer: Muhammad, Ibrahim, Musa, Isa, Nuh. (Quran 46:35, 33:7)

label: Sunnah · Prophet
question: 23 years of prophethood — how was it divided between Makkah and Madinah?
answer: 13 years Makkah (610-622 CE), 10 years Madinah (622-632 CE). Born ~570 CE, died age 63.

label: Sunnah · Prophet
question: What happened at Uhud (3 AH)? What key lesson did the Muslims learn?
answer: Archers left posts, Quraysh counter-attacked. 70 martyred including Hamza. Lesson: obey the Prophet's commands. (Quran 3:152)

label: Sunnah · Prophet
question: What was the Farewell Sermon? Name three key messages.
answer: Arafah, final Hajj (10 AH). All people equal by taqwa, riba abolished, treat women well, hold to Quran and Sunnah.

label: Sunnah · Prophet
question: Name the miracle of Musa AS, Isa AS, and Muhammad (pbuh).
answer: Musa: parting Red Sea. Isa: raising the dead. Muhammad: the Quran itself.

label: Sunnah · Prophet
question: What is the Year of Sorrow ('Aam al-Huzn)? What two losses occurred?
answer: ~619 CE. Lost Khadijah (wife) and Abu Talib (uncle/protector) within weeks.

label: Ummah · Pillars
question: Name all FIVE Pillars of Islam in order.
answer: 1) Shahadah 2) Salah 3) Zakat 4) Sawm 5) Hajj. (Bukhari 8)

label: Ummah · Pillars
question: Name the five daily prayers in order.
answer: Fajr · Dhuhr · Asr · Maghrib · Isha

label: Ummah · Pillars
question: What month is Ramadan? Why is it significant?
answer: 9th Islamic month. Quran revealed (2:185). Fasting obligatory. Month of Laylat al-Qadr.

label: Ummah · Pillars
question: What is Hajj? When is it performed and who must do it?
answer: Pilgrimage to Makkah in Dhul Hijjah. Obligatory once for every able Muslim.

label: Ummah · Pillars
question: What do Muslims say when they hear the Prophet (pbuh)'s name?
answer: Sallallahu alayhi wa sallam — 'May Allah's peace and blessings be upon him.' (Quran 33:56)

label: Ummah · Pillars
question: What is the Adhan? Who calls it and what does 'Allahu Akbar' mean?
answer: Call to prayer. Called by Muaddhin. 'Allahu Akbar' = 'Allah is the Greatest.'

label: Ummah · Sahaba
question: Who was the FIRST person ever to accept Islam?
answer: Khadijah bint Khuwaylid — the Prophet's first wife.

label: Ummah · Sahaba
question: Who was the first free adult man to accept Islam? What title was he given?
answer: Abu Bakr As-Siddiq — 'The Truthful One.' First Caliph.

label: Ummah · Sahaba
question: Which Sahabi was called 'Dhul Nurayn' (possessor of two lights) and why?
answer: Uthman ibn Affan — married two daughters of the Prophet. 3rd Caliph.

label: Ummah · Sahaba
question: Who was the first child to accept Islam?
answer: Ali ibn Abi Talib — the Prophet's cousin, later 4th Caliph. About 10 years old.

label: Ummah · Sahaba
question: Who was Sumayyah bint Khayyat and why is she significant?
answer: First martyr (shaheedah) in Islam. Killed by Abu Jahl for refusing to renounce faith.

label: Ummah · Pillars
question: Recite the Shahadah and explain what it means.
answer: 'Ash-hadu an la ilaha illa Allah, wa ash-hadu anna Muhammadan rasulullah.' — No deity except Allah, Muhammad is His messenger.

label: Ummah · Pillars
question: Name the SIX Articles of Faith (Arkan al-Iman).
answer: 1) Allah 2) Angels 3) Books 4) Messengers 5) Last Day 6) Divine Decree (Qadar). (Muslim 8)

label: Ummah · Pillars
question: What is Zakat? Who must pay it and at what rate?
answer: Obligatory almsgiving. 2.5% of savings above nisab (~85g gold) held for one lunar year.

label: Ummah · Pillars
question: What is Wudu? Name the four fardh acts.
answer: 1) Wash face 2) Arms to elbows 3) Wipe head 4) Feet to ankles. (Quran 5:6)

label: Ummah · Pillars
question: What is Tahajjud prayer? When is the best time?
answer: Voluntary night prayer after Isha before Fajr. Best: last third of the night. (Bukhari 1145)

label: Ummah · Pillars
question: What is I'tikaf and when is it commonly practiced?
answer: Spiritual retreat in the mosque, last 10 nights of Ramadan. Seeking Laylat al-Qadr.

label: Ummah · Sahaba
question: Name the Four Rightly-Guided Caliphs in order.
answer: 1) Abu Bakr 2) Umar ibn al-Khattab 3) Uthman ibn Affan 4) Ali ibn Abi Talib

label: Ummah · Sahaba
question: Who was Bilal ibn Rabah and why is he significant?
answer: Former Abyssinian slave. First Muaddhin. Freed by Abu Bakr. Symbol of equality.

label: Ummah · Sahaba
question: Who was Aisha bint Abi Bakr? Name three facts.
answer: 1) Wife of Prophet, Mother of Believers 2) ~2,210 hadiths 3) Greatest female scholar.

label: Ummah · Sahaba
question: Which Sahabi was 'Saifullah' (Sword of Allah)? Why?
answer: Khalid ibn al-Walid — undefeated commander. Title given after Battle of Mu'tah (8 AH).

label: Ummah · Sahaba
question: Who was Umar ibn al-Khattab? Name two things he is famous for.
answer: 2nd Caliph. 1) Justice — walked streets at night 2) Expanded state to Jerusalem, Persia, Egypt.

label: Ummah · Pillars
question: Name four conditions for Salah to be valid.
answer: 1) Wudu 2) Clean body/clothes/place 3) Cover awra 4) Face qibla 5) Prayer time 6) Niyya

label: Ummah · Pillars
question: Name the four Sunni madhahib and their founders.
answer: 1) Hanafi — Abu Hanifa 2) Maliki — Malik ibn Anas 3) Shafi'i — Ash-Shafi'i 4) Hanbali — Ahmad ibn Hanbal

label: Ummah · Pillars
question: What is the difference between Sadaqah and Zakat?
answer: Zakat: obligatory 2.5%, specific recipients (9:60). Sadaqah: voluntary, no fixed amount, includes kind acts.

label: Ummah · Pillars
question: Name the rites of Hajj in order (at least 5 key steps).
answer: 1) Ihram 2) Tawaf 3) Sa'i 4) Arafah 5) Muzdalifah 6) Jamarat 7) Sacrifice 8) Tawaf al-Ifadah 9) Farewell Tawaf

label: Ummah · Pillars
question: What is Tayammum? When permitted and how performed?
answer: Dry ablution with clean earth. When water unavailable. Strike earth, wipe face, wipe hands. (Quran 4:43)

label: Ummah · Pillars
question: Name the four sacred months when fighting was forbidden.
answer: Dhul Qa'dah (11th), Dhul Hijjah (12th), Muharram (1st), Rajab (7th). (Quran 9:36)

label: Ummah · Sahaba
question: Name the Ten Companions promised Paradise.
answer: Abu Bakr, Umar, Uthman, Ali, Talha, Zubair, Abdurrahman ibn Awf, Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, Sa'id ibn Zayd, Abu Ubayda.

label: Ummah · Sahaba
question: Who compiled the first Quran? Who standardised it?
answer: Zaid ibn Thabit under Abu Bakr. Standardised by Uthman ibn Affan. After Battle of Yamama.

label: Ummah · Sahaba
question: Who was Salman al-Farsi? What strategy did he suggest at the Battle of the Trench?
answer: Persian companion. Suggested digging a trench around Madinah (5 AH). Saved city from 10,000 soldiers.

label: Ummah · Sahaba
question: Who was Mus'ab ibn Umayr? Why is he the 'first ambassador of Islam'?
answer: Wealthy Qurayshi who gave up luxury. Sent to Madinah before Hijra to teach. Martyred at Uhud.

label: Ummah · Sahaba
question: Who was Fatimah? Name three facts about her significance.
answer: 1) Daughter of Prophet and Khadijah 2) Wife of Ali, mother of Hasan and Husayn 3) 'Leader of Women of Paradise.' (Bukhari 3624)
